h2h_summary: Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina have never met before; this World Cup group stage match will be the first official encounter between the two nations.
key_players_home: Jonathan David (striker, all-time top scorer with 39 international goals)
Alphonso Davies (left back/captain, recovering from hamstring injury, doubtful for opener)
key_players_away: Edin Dzeko (striker/captain, 73 international goals, all-time top scorer)
Esmir Bajraktarevic (winger, PSV Eindhoven, scored winning WC qualifier penalty vs Italy)
team_style_home: Canada play an intense high-press 4-4-2 / 4-2-3-1 system known as 'Maplepressing' under Jesse Marsch, winning the ball high up the pitch and launching rapid vertical attacks with a high defensive line.
team_style_away: Bosnia and Herzegovina deploy a physical and direct 4-2-3-1 under Sergej Barbarez, defending compactly and hitting quickly on the counter, with direct service into Dzeko and wide play through the flanks.
wc_history_home: Canada have appeared at only two World Cups before 2026: in 1986 (exited in the group stage without scoring) and 2022 (group stage exit, but scored their historic first World Cup goal via Alphonso Davies against Croatia).
wc_history_away: Bosnia and Herzegovina's only previous World Cup appearance was in 2014, where they were eliminated in the group stage despite a memorable 3-1 win over Iran in a group that also included Argentina and Nigeria.
group_context: Group B features Canada (co-host, ranked 27th), Switzerland (ranked 17th and group favourites), Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Qatar; Canada desperately need points in front of their home crowd while Bosnia, having qualified dramatically by beating Italy on penalties, are targeting a surprise run to the knockout rounds.
strengths_home: Home crowd advantage at BMO Field in Toronto
High-intensity pressing system that unsettles opponents in their own half
Potent attacking duo of Jonathan David and Cyle Larin
strengths_away: Veteran leadership of Edin Dzeko providing a reliable target and clinical finishing
Battle-hardened squad tempered by dramatic penalty shootout wins over Wales and Italy in qualifying
Disciplined counter-attacking style that can exploit space left by pressing teams
